I spent $89 on a dorm chair and bins for my sister. Four hours before check-in ended, it was the single best purchase in the car.
The dorm move-in mistake that costs you 45 minutes: unpacking the closet before you've dealt with under-bed storage. Do the bins first.
Dorm desk chairs are usually the ugliest object in the room. Found one in velvet that folds flat, bundled with storage bins, for $89.
Honest flaw in the $89 dorm bin set: the zippers. I'd give them 1-2 years of daily use, not five. Still worth it for the price.
My sister's dorm desk had one dim overhead light and a badly placed outlet. Fixed the whole corner for $25 with one lamp.
Nothing says 'temporary institution' louder than bare dorm windows. $28 in blackout curtains on a tension rod (no drilling) fixed it in 15 minutes.
A $22 diffuser was the last thing I bought for the dorm room, and it's the one thing that changed how the whole room felt in ten minutes.
Full dorm move-in cost, itemized: $89 chair and bins, $32 extra bins, $25 lamp, $40 curtains and lights, $22 diffuser. $208 total, all in.
Is a $89 folding velvet dorm chair actually comfortable? For sessions under 2 hours, yes. For a 6-hour study grind, I wouldn't count on it.
Tight dorm budget before August? Skip everything else and just get the $89 chair-and-bin combo. It did more work than the rest of the car combined.
